name: hswm-exploratory-cli-provider-transport
purpose: Adapt one bounded Claude, Grok, or Codex headless invocation to the existing HSWM OpenAI-compatible transport seam while preserving process identity, isolation strength, exact output, and a tamper-evident terminal receipt.
planned_consumers: future local broker only after measured multi-client demand
rationale: One current callable consumer does not justify a queue, scheduler, HTTP daemon, or authoritative mutable-state owner.
The installed CLIs already own authentication, quota, session mechanics, and provider-specific execution; HSWM should not impersonate those control planes.
A stateless adapter provides the useful API-shaped seam now without moving live calls into the deterministic world compiler.
Provider-managed CLI model identity is weaker than a content-attested local deployment, so it must remain a separately labelled exploratory arm.

boundary.owns: Request validation, provider-specific isolated command construction, bounded subprocess lifecycle, structured-output validation, OpenAI-compatible envelope adaptation, and immutable CLI invocation receipts
boundary.mechanism: Execute exactly one direct CLI child with shell disabled, a temporary HOME containing only provider authentication, provider-specific capability denial, bounded output and retention, process-group cancellation, provider-native JSON schema, and local exact schema validation
policy_outside: Which exploratory questions or datasets may call a subscription-backed CLI
Provider account selection, authentication, quota, billing, and terms
Retries, scheduling, durable queues, HTTP exposure, and multi-tenant admission
Promotion of any CLI output into a frozen HSWM observation bundle
Confirmatory H3-B3 model selection and deployment attestation
non_goals: Replacing OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, or local-vLLM APIs
Serving a network API or retaining long-lived sessions
Running write-capable agents, MCP servers, web search, repository tools, or subagents
Claiming content-attested model identity from a provider-managed CLI alias
Feeding CLI output into the pinned Qwen H3-B3 confirmatory manifest
boundary.state_authority: The module owns no durable mutable state. One in-process instance temporarily owns active child handles and terminal receipts; the caller owns persistence and publication.

contract: make_cli_transport(CLIProviderConfigV1, OutputContract) -> CLIProviderTransport; transport(OpenAIRequestV1) -> TransportResponseV1 | CLITransportError(receipt)
invariants: The shell is never invoked and user prompt content never becomes a command-line argument.

Claude exposes no tools; Grok runs with no configured external capability and a deny-all rule but honestly labels its inert MCP meta-tool surface; Codex is refused unless the caller explicitly accepts soft read-only isolation.
Every terminal path records provider, requested model, identity strength, executable hash, CLI version, public argv, exact stdout bytes, stderr hash, input and schema hashes, timing, and a self-hash.
Only a zero-exit, exact UTF-8 JSON result that passes the selected local contract becomes an OpenAI-compatible success envelope.
Timeout, cancellation, output limit, nonzero exit, duplicate JSON keys, and schema rejection terminate fail closed.
No credential or environment value is serialized into a receipt; only the names of inherited variables are recorded.
Each provider inherits only its own credential class; HOME and provider config directories are temporary and transport-controlled.
Temperature, seed, top-p, message-role priority, and max-token parity are requested but explicitly recorded as not CLI-enforced.
The executable content hash is rechecked immediately before every spawn, and in-memory receipt retention is bounded.
The module never mutates compiler, manifest, preflight, falsifier, or H3 result artifacts.
failure_model: Missing executable or failed version probe rejects transport construction.
Queue wait and child execution share one caller-visible deadline.
Cancellation and timeout signal the entire child process group TERM then KILL after a bounded grace period.
Stdout or stderr overflow immediately terminates the group and publishes an OUTPUT_LIMIT receipt.
All non-success terminal states raise CLITransportError carrying the validated receipt; no partial result is returned.
verification: Fake CLI contract tests cover Claude, Grok, and opt-in Codex command shapes and OpenAI envelope mapping.

Tests cover duplicate keys and non-finite JSON numbers, invalid schema, nonzero exit, timeout, explicit cancellation, parent-interrupt, descendant, and unexpected-capture cleanup, stdout and stderr limits, concurrency and retention bounds, receipt tampering, executable drift, provider-specific credentials, ephemeral homes, and secret non-recording.
An integration test exercises the unchanged recorded_llm_extractor.Transport callable seam.
The engine-decision validator checks this module verdict artifact.
promotion_gates: At least two independent current clients require one stable remote protocol rather than an in-process callable.
Measured operations require durable jobs, idempotency keys, bounded queues, restart recovery, and explicit backpressure.
A real service owner, authentication policy, bind policy, quota policy, and schema migration obligation exist.
Provider terms and supported automation surfaces have been reviewed for the intended deployment context.
A producer-identity contract separates CLI provenance from hswm-recorded-openai-compatible/v1 without invalidating confirmatory artifacts.
falsifiers: If a provider cannot maintain the documented no-tool isolation, disable that provider rather than weakening the receipt label.
If CLI output or model identity cannot be reproduced well enough for an exploratory comparison, use the provider's official API instead.
If no HSWM consumer uses the callable seam after one development cycle, keep the module unpromoted or archive it.
If a network broker would merely wrap one local caller, do not manufacture an engine boundary.
